name: which
usage_summary: Used in three distinct call sites across cli-kit and app. (1) cli-kit/src/public/node/system.ts: which.sync() to check if a command resolves to a local directory binary before executing it (security guard). (2) cli-kit/src/public/node/version.ts: which.sync('shopify', {all: true}) to find all globally-installed shopify binaries. (3) app/src/cli/utilities/mkcert.ts: which('mkcert', {nothrow: true}) to check if mkcert is already on PATH before downloading it.
how_to_remove: Node 18.19+ provides import('node:child_process').execFileSync with which-like PATH resolution, but a direct drop-in is `node:child_process` + manual PATH splitting. Alternatively the lightweight `locate-path` or a hand-rolled PATH scan using `fs.accessSync` could replace all three call sites. The total surface area is small — three call sites with well-understood semantics.
replacement_reason: All three usages (sync PATH lookup, find-all PATH lookup, nothrow PATH lookup) can be implemented with a small helper using Node's built-in `node:child_process` / `node:fs` / `process.env.PATH` without adding a new dependency.
